2007-12-01.title: Does It Play in Punjab?

2007-12-01.abstract: After 18 months of unproductive talks between the Europeans and the Iranians, the London meeting was a last-ditch attempt to persuade Iran to compromise.

2007-12-01.abstract: Zhou Zhengyi was pronounced guilty on five charges, including misappropriation of funds, bribery and forging tax receipts, state media reported.

2007-12-02.abstract: Serbs have used satire and dark humor to come to terms with decades of authoritarian rule. Now, the old art form is having a renaissance.

2007-12-03.title: White House Reaction to Iran Report
2007-12-03.abstract: Here is the text of a statement issued today by Stephen Hadley, President Bush’s national security adviser, concerning the new National Intelligence Estimate on Iran’s nuclear weapons program. The intelligence estimate says that, contrary to previous assessments, Iran halted the program in 2003 in response to international pressure and has probably not restarted it since then, though it has the capacity to do so.

2007-12-03.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates arrived in the Horn of Africa to inspect an installment built to wage peace.

The United Nations said that all but three of the dead — a Dane, a Filipino and a Senegalese — were thought to be Algerians.

2007-12-27.abstract: The defense minister of Israel, Ehud Barak, met on Wednesday with Egypt’s President Hosni Mubarak in the Sinai resort of Sharm el Sheik.

2007-12-27.title: 4 Sunni Allies of U.S. Killed in Iraq
2007-12-27.abstract: Four members of a local American-backed Awakening group in Diyala Province were killed Wednesday when a house they were raiding exploded, the police said.

2007-12-28.title: Landmark Election in Kenya
2007-12-28.abstract: Millions of Kenyans voted in a presidential election that is predicted to be the tightest race in the country's history.

2007-12-28.title: Kenyans Vote in Test of Democracy
2007-12-28.abstract: The presidential election is predicted to be the tightest race in the country’s history — and perhaps the greatest test yet of Kenya’s young, multiparty democracy.

2007-12-28.title: Kenyans Vote in Test of Democracy
2007-12-28.abstract: The presidential election is predicted to be the tightest race in the country’s history.

2007-12-28.title: Malaysia: Hindu Loses Bid to Block Son’s Conversion
2007-12-28.abstract: Malaysia’s highest court rejected a Hindu woman’s plea to stop her Muslim-convert husband from changing their 4-year-old son’s religion to Islam.

2007-12-28.title: Mudslides Claim at Least 80 Lives in Indonesia
2007-12-28.abstract: Rescue workers struggled on Thursday to move emergency equipment into the mountainous area, which has been difficult to reach.

2007-12-29.title: With Half of Vote Counted, Kenyan Opposition Is Poised to Sweep
2007-12-29.abstract: A populist challenger is likely to unseat the incumbent president, according to preliminary election results.

2007-12-29.title: Nigeria Reassigns Top Corruption Fighter
2007-12-29.abstract: Nigeria’s top anticorruption official is being sent to a yearlong course at a remote training institute, provoking criticism from many who described the move as an attempt to sideline him.

2007-12-29.title: Kenya Erupts in Violence as Rivals Declare Victory
2007-12-29.abstract: Supporters of the leading presidential challenger were protesting what they said was a plot by the government to steal the closely contested election.

2007-12-29.title: Car Bomber Kills 8 at a Street Market in Baghdad
2007-12-29.abstract: The bomber timed the blast to catch people emerging from prayers on the Muslim holy day and set off the explosives directly under a mural celebrating doves of peace.

2007-12-29.title: Convicted Charity Workers Return to France From Chad
2007-12-29.abstract: The workers now face an effort by the French courts to adjust their sentences of eight years of hard labor.

2007-12-30.title: Violence in Karachi
2007-12-30.abstract: Sindh Province, one of Pakistan's three ethnic minority provinces and home to the family graveyard of Benazir Bhutto, has been the site of the worst violence in the wake of Ms. Bhutto's death.

2007-12-30.title: Tapes by C.I.A. Lived and Died to Save Image
2007-12-30.abstract: Decisions about interrogation videotapes were prompted by fear about what the public might think, officials say.

2007-12-30.title: Bhutto’s Son and Husband to Lead Party
2007-12-30.abstract: Benazir Bhutto’s son, 19, said that his mother’s party, the biggest and most potent in Pakistan, will contest the coming election.

2007-12-30.title: In Bhutto Stronghold, Sharif Seeks a Political Alliance Against Musharraf
2007-12-30.abstract: Nawaz Sharif needs to forge an alliance with Benazir Bhutto’s political party to challenge the government of President Pervez Musharraf.

2007-12-30.title: Tribal Rivalry Boils Over After Kenyan Election
2007-12-30.abstract: President Mwai Kibaki was declared the winner amid riots in Kenya’s slums and evidence of election mischief.

2007-12-31.title: Kenya Erupts in Violence
2007-12-31.abstract: The most fiercely fought election in Kenya's history has pitched one of the most stable nations in Africa toward chaos.

2007-12-31.title: U.S. Experts Criticize Bhutto Post-Mortem
2007-12-31.abstract: Leading American experts in forensic pathology deplored the failure of Pakistani officials to order an autopsy of Benazir Bhutto, saying it was a crucial part of any credible investigation of a murder.

2007-12-31.title: In Prison, Toddlers Serve Time With Mom
2007-12-31.abstract: Fifty-three children under the age of 6 live inside a prison in Mexico City with their mothers, who are serving sentences for crimes from drug dealing to kidnapping to homicide.

2007-12-31.title: New Questions Arise in Killing of Ex-Premier
2007-12-31.abstract: Indications that doctors felt pressured to conform to government accounts of Benazir Bhutto’s death added to the pressure for an international inquiry.

2007-12-31.title: U.N. Force Takes Control in Darfur
2007-12-31.abstract: The United Nations officially began a new peace-keeping effort in a ceremony that transferred power from the African Union.
